text.title: Monolith Architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Business logic lives in controllers; domain layer has only data classes.
antipattern.root-cause: Layered chosen by default; rules of dependency direction not enforced.
antipattern.fix: Adopt vertical-slice OR enforce domain → application → infrastructure dependency direction in CI.

antipattern.symptom: BaseService / BaseController hold cross-cutting logic; every feature inherits.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineers introduced inheritance to share code without thinking about coupling.
antipattern.fix: Move cross-cutting concerns to middleware or composition; ban `Base*` classes in code review.

antipattern.symptom: Order creation implemented as a 5-step saga inside a single monolith.
antipattern.root-cause: r3 ignored; ACID abandoned without distributed deploy.
antipattern.fix: Collapse into a single transaction; reserve sagas for true cross-service flows.

antipattern.symptom: Service extracted from big-ball-of-mud monolith; service still calls the monolith DB directly.
antipattern.root-cause: r5 ignored; extraction attempted without prior modularity.
antipattern.fix: Roll back extraction; convert to modular monolith first; extract only when bounded.
